reviewed_items.reviewer_notes: Code reading is factually correct: verify_cell_kzg_proof_batch (eip7594.c:802-952) does not validate array lengths before indexing at lines 838-840, 858, and 894-896. However, this is a latent library API issue, not a current vulnerability. All examined language bindings (Go:548, Node.js:877-879, Python:637-650, Java:683-701, Rust:528-548) validate array lengths before calling the C function, preventing out-of-bounds access. The c-kzg-4844 library is designed to be called exclusively through these validated bindings in the Ethereum client ecosystem; no direct C/C++ callers exist in production clients. Per 01e_PARTIAL_W2B3_1771748669.json lines 931-947, the invariant requires 'len(commitments) == len(cell_indices) == len(cells) == len(proofs)', which is enforced at the binding layer, not the C layer. This is a library design choice (thin C API, thick binding validation) rather than an exploitable vulnerability. Severity calibrated to Informational per BUG_BOUNTY_SCOPE.json: no direct security risk, only applicable if maintainers decide to add C-level validation.
reviewed_items.spec_reference: 01e_PARTIAL_W2B3_1771748669.json property PROP-6a4369e9-inv-037: 'verify_cell_kzg_proof_batch must receive consistent-length arrays for valid verification. len(commitments) == len(cell_indices) == len(cells) == len(proofs)'
